message:
fix(ft-api): poisson_nll_loss composes through autograd primitives [bd-zl3n]

First child of frankentorch-2med (severed-autograd loss umbrella).
poisson_nll_loss used to extract values, compute the per-element
formula in plain f64, build a requires_grad=false leaf, then take
tensor_mean — the tensor_mean preserved autograd from there but
the prior leaf had already severed it, so input never received any
gradient.

Compose through autograd-aware primitives:
  log_input=true:  loss_i = exp(input_i) - target_i * input_i
  log_input=false: loss_i = input_i - target_i * log(input_i + eps)
  loss = mean(per_elem)

The Stirling correction (full=true) is target * log(target) -
target + 0.5 * log(2π * target) — a function of target only with
no input gradient. Precompute its mean as a non-grad scalar and
add via tensor_add. PyTorch only adds when target > 1; we
replicate that.

Adds poisson_nll_loss_propagates_gradient_through_input regression
test: input=[1, 2], target=[1, 1] (log_input=true) →
analytical [(e-1)/2, (e^2-1)/2] = [0.8591..., 3.1945...].
Asserts to 1e-12.

Closes frankentorch-zl3n. ft-api lib tests: 1157 passing (+1).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:

 {"id":"frankentorch-1s2","title":"Add F.pad reflect/replicate/circular modes","description":"Extend tensor_pad to support reflect, replicate, circular padding modes. Currently only constant padding. Essential for vision preprocessing.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-02T19:49:10.441649598Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T19:53:41.804652781Z","closed_at":"2026-04-02T19:53:41.804379905Z","close_reason":"Implemented tensor_pad_mode with reflect, replicate, circular modes. 6 tests passing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-1tax","title":"Statistical / special ops with severed-autograd: cov, corrcoef, mode, quantile, logit, erfinv, digamma, polygamma, multigammaln, entr, xlogy, xlog1py, logcumsumexp","description":"These ft-api ops all extract tensor values via self.tensor_values + return self.tensor_tape.leaf(... false), silently severing the autograd tape on tracked inputs (the same pattern recently fixed for SELU / CELU / Threshold / Hardtanh / Unfold / Fold / index_* / scatter_* / masked_scatter / PReLU + 6 shrink-family activations). Some have explicit fail-loud guards (logit doesn't, erfinv does, digamma doesn't, etc.) but the contract is inconsistent. Pick each off and either compose through autograd primitives (preferred where the math admits it) or add a uniform fail-loud guard. PyTorch's torch.cov / torch.corrcoef / torch.special.{logit,digamma,polygamma,...} are all differentiable. Children: each op gets its own fix bead.","notes":"All children resolved across multiple commits: cov/corrcoef/mode/quantile/logit/xlogy/xlog1py/entr composed through autograd primitives in earlier rounds; gammaln (7cc0b45), digamma (86fb29d), polygamma + multigammaln (bd2817b), erfinv (ac049c6), logcumsumexp (ec75d35) wrapped in tensor_apply_function with analytical backwards. ft-api lib tests now at 1135 passing.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-29T20:24:23.438424203Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-29T21:29:34.517650830Z","closed_at":"2026-04-29T21:29:34.515232671Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-2er","title":"Add custom autograd Function API (torch.autograd.Function equivalent)","description":"Users cannot currently define custom backward functions. This is arguably the single most impactful missing feature for real-world use — without it, any layer whose backward isn't built into FrankenTorch's op set is impossible to implement.

PyTorch's torch.autograd.Function API allows users to:
1. Define a custom forward() that registers itself on the autograd tape
2. Define a custom backward() that computes gradients for the custom op
3. Save tensors for backward via ctx.save_for_backward()

Implementation plan:
1. Add a CustomFunction trait in ft-autograd with forward/backward methods
2. Add a TensorNodeOp::Custom variant to the tensor tape
3. Add session.apply_custom_function() to ft-api that invokes the user's forward, registers the op on the tape, and stores the backward closure
4. During backward traversal, call the user's backward function when encountering Custom nodes
5. Handle save_for_backward by capturing TensorNodeIds in the closure context

Key design decisions:
- The backward closure must receive upstream gradient and return downstream gradients
- Must respect no_grad context (custom forward inside no_grad should not record on tape)
- Must work with the existing deterministic backward scheduler
- Error handling: user backward functions should return Result, not panic

Tests:
- Unit test: custom ReLU equivalent (forward: max(0,x), backward: 1 if x>0 else 0)
- Unit test: custom function with multiple inputs/outputs
- Unit test: custom function inside no_grad context (should not record)
- Unit test: custom function backward produces correct gradients (numerical gradient check)
- E2E test: train a model with a custom layer and verify loss decreases
- Edge case: nested custom functions
- Edge case: custom function that saves tensors for backward","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:16:46.006446323Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T20:15:34.162735830Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T20:15:34.162441333Z","close_reason":"Core functionality already exists: tensor_apply_function() with FunctionCtx (save_for_backward, needs_input_grad) + backward closure. Extensive tests in ft-autograd (lines 17484+) and used internally by ft-api for ~12 ops. The closure-based API is the Rust-idiomatic equivalent of PyTorch's class-based torch.autograd.Function. A trait wrapper could be added later for API sugar but is not blocking.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankentorch-2med","title":"Severed-autograd loss umbrella: focal, poisson_nll, gaussian_nll","description":"Three more loss functions in ft-api that extract values, compute the per-element loss in plain f64, build a requires_grad=false leaf, then take tensor_mean — same severed-autograd pattern recently fixed for cross_entropy_with_smoothing / multilabel_soft_margin / soft_margin / multi_margin. The tensor_mean step preserves autograd from that point but the prior leaf has already severed the tape — so input never receives gradient.

Affected:
  - focal_loss (lib.rs:10324) — used in dense object detection (RetinaNet etc.)
  - poisson_nll_loss (lib.rs:10357) — count regression
  - gaussian_nll_loss (lib.rs:10398) — variational / probabilistic regression

Each has a closed-form composition through autograd-aware primitives; Stirling / 2pi correction terms are constants and can be added as scalars without affecting the tape.

Children: each loss gets its own fix bead. Surfaced during continuing /reality-check pass.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-30T21:39:48.922194244Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-30T21:39:48.922194244Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-2os","title":"Implement slogdet, pinverse, kthvalue, linalg_cross, linalg_vector_norm","description":"Add numerically stable linalg and selection ops: slogdet (sign+log determinant), pinverse (SVD-based pseudoinverse), kthvalue (k-th smallest), linalg_cross (3D cross product), linalg_vector_norm (flexible vector norm). Used in scientific computing, regularization, and model analysis.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-28T14:15:54.437563761Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-29T02:09:46.045687331Z","closed_at":"2026-03-29T02:09:46.045431557Z","close_reason":"slogdet/cross already existed. Implemented pinverse (SVD-based), kthvalue (with index), linalg_vector_norm (L0/L1/L2/Linf). 9 tests passing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-2rl","title":"Wire f32 tensor comparisons and float classify to dispatch","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-11T04:57:24.571301561Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-11T05:01:04.720640114Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T05:01:04.720320760Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-2xxj","title":"Activation functions lack subprocess conformance: sigmoid, tanh, silu, mish","description":"Existing subprocess conformance suite covers gelu, softplus, exp/log/expm1/log1p/erf/erfc/lgamma/trig/hyperbolic, but the workhorse activations sigmoid / tanh / silu / mish have no scipy or libm reference comparison. PyTorch parity for these matters because:
- sigmoid (= 1/(1+exp(-x)) = expit) is the building block for binary classification, gating, attention
- tanh is the workhorse for RNN/LSTM
- silu/swish (= x*sigmoid(x)) is used in transformer FFN
- mish (= x*tanh(softplus(x))) is used in detection / vision

Reference: scipy.special.expit (sigmoid), numpy.tanh, numpy compositions for silu and mish. ULP-tolerant comparison; FrankenTorch composes these through libm exp/tanh which should match within ~1 ULP on smooth interior plus edge cases for ±inf / NaN / large-magnitude saturation.

Add torch_activations_libm_subprocess_conformance covering all four with ~30 inputs spanning negative / zero / positive / large / Inf / NaN. Filed during testing-conformance pass.","notes":"Fixed in this commit. Added torch_activations_scipy_subprocess_conformance covering sigmoid (vs scipy.special.expit), tanh (vs numpy.tanh), silu = x*sigmoid(x), and mish = x*tanh(softplus(x)) across 30 inputs spanning negative / zero / positive / saturation (±50, ±100, ±700) / Inf / NaN. ULP-tolerant comparator: 4 ULP for sigmoid/tanh, 16 ULP for the composed silu/mish. Subprocess conformance count now 33 (was 32).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-30T21:09:45.897091490Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-30T21:12:17.783694467Z","closed_at":"2026-04-30T21:12:17.780442649Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"frankentorch-zdpm","title":"masked_scatter all-false mask skips source validation","description":"Reviewer finding in crates/ft-api/src/lib.rs live masked_scatter rewrite. tensor_masked_scatter now returns Ok(input) when n_true == 0 before validating source dtype/device compatibility. Repro from code inspection: call tensor_masked_scatter with an F64 input, an all-zero mask with matching numel, and an incompatible source tensor such as F32. The new fast path returns the input unchanged instead of failing closed; with any true mask the path reaches tensor_where and rejects mismatched dtypes. This makes validation data-dependent on mask contents and can silently accept unsupported source tensors. Fix: validate source dtype/device against input before the n_true == 0 fast path, and add a regression test for all-false mask plus incompatible source.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-29T16:44:37.285274513Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-29T17:26:21.156330292Z","closed_at":"2026-04-29T17:26:21.154523481Z","close_reason":"Hoisted dtype + device validation for input/mask/source above the n_true==0 fast path in tensor_masked_scatter, so the fast and slow paths share the same up-front contract regardless of mask contents. Added masked_scatter_all_false_mask_still_validates_source_dtype regression test.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-zes","title":"Standardize top-level oracle references to repo-relative mirror wording","description":"AGENTS.md, PLAN_TO_PORT_PYTORCH_TO_RUST.md, and COMPREHENSIVE_SPEC_FOR_FRANKENTORCH_V1.md still use absolute example roots for the legacy oracle mirror. Update them to repo-relative wording (`legacy_pytorch_code/pytorch`) so they remain correct across /data and /dp checkouts.","notes":"Standardized the top-level oracle references in AGENTS.md, PLAN_TO_PORT_PYTORCH_TO_RUST.md, and COMPREHENSIVE_SPEC_FOR_FRANKENTORCH_V1.md to repo-relative mirror wording (`legacy_pytorch_code/pytorch`) so the docs no longer depend on /dp vs /data checkout prefixes.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"docs","created_at":"2026-03-28T05:36:08.944450919Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-28T05:36:38.063635010Z","closed_at":"2026-03-28T05:36:38.063008454Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankentorch-zl3n","title":"Fix poisson_nll_loss autograd via tensor primitives [child of 2med]","description":"poisson_nll_loss at lib.rs:10357 computes elementwise:
  log_input=True:  loss_i = exp(inp_i) - tgt_i * inp_i
  log_input=False: loss_i = inp_i - tgt_i * log(inp_i + eps)
plus optional Stirling correction tgt*log(tgt) - tgt + 0.5*log(2*pi*tgt) for full=true.

Composable through autograd primitives — the Stirling correction is a function of target only (no input gradient) and can be precomputed as a non-grad scalar tensor and added back in.","notes":"Fixed in this commit. Composed poisson_nll_loss through autograd primitives:
  log_input=true:  exp(input) - target * input → mean
  log_input=false: input - target * log(input + eps) → mean
The Stirling correction (full=true) depends only on target, so we precompute its mean as a non-grad scalar and add via tensor_add. Added poisson_nll_loss_propagates_gradient_through_input regression test (input=[1,2], target=[1,1] → analytical [(e-1)/2, (e^2-1)/2]). ft-api lib tests: 1157.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-30T21:39:55.547701335Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-30T21:42:04.586823306Z","closed_at":"2026-04-30T21:42:04.584446394Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-zo2","title":"Harden unfold/renorm parameter validation","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-11T18:12:12.008665624Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-11T18:12:25.047698960Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T18:12:25.047522837Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-zs8a","title":"tensor_linalg_svd doesn't complete orthonormal basis for zero singular vectors","description":"On rank-deficient matrices, FrankenTorch's tensor_linalg_svd returns U columns corresponding to zero singular values as all-zeros instead of orthonormal vectors completing the basis. numpy.linalg.svd (and torch.linalg.svd) always produce a fully orthonormal U with U^T U = I_k, regardless of rank. Repro: 3x3 matrix [[1,2,3],[4,5,6],[7,8,9]] (rank 2). numpy returns U[:,2] as a unit vector orthogonal to col([1,2,3],[4,5,6]); FrankenTorch returns U[:,2] = 0. Surfaced by torch_linalg_svd_numpy_subprocess_conformance during the frankentorch-c36b work; current conformance test trims the rank-deficient case to land. Severity: medium — breaks the U^T U = I invariant that downstream code (e.g., least-squares via SVD) may rely on; doesn't affect the U @ diag(S) @ Vh reconstruction.","notes":"Fixed in this commit: unified the orthonormal-basis completion in svd_tall to handle both (a) rank-deficient inputs (zero singular values) and (b) full_matrices mode with m > k. Any column of U that ends up with norm <= tol now gets a unit vector orthogonal to all previously-set columns via Gram-Schmidt against e_0..e_{m-1}, retrying basis vectors as needed. Restored the rank_deficient_3x3 case in both torch_linalg_svd_numpy_subprocess_conformance and torch_linalg_pinv_numpy_subprocess_conformance — both pass.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-29T22:03:37.112373139Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-29T22:20:10.300571314Z","closed_at":"2026-04-29T22:20:10.298049888Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-zvq","title":"Fail closed on fractional bincount inputs","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-29T01:57:33.570133221Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-29T01:59:23.253969202Z","closed_at":"2026-03-29T01:59:23.253771318Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
